name: Canning Asparagus (Spears or Pieces)
recipeIngredient: An average of 24-1/2 pounds is needed per canner load of 7 quarts; an average of 16 pounds is needed per canner load of 9 pints. A crate weighs 31 pounds and yields 7 to 12 quarts--an average of 3-1/2 pounds per quart.
recipeInstructions:
1. Quality: Use tender, tight-tipped spears, 4 to 6 inches long.
2. Procedure: Wash asparagus and trim off tough scales. Break off tough stems and wash again. Cut into 1-inch pieces or can whole.
3. Hot pack: Cover asparagus with boiling water. Boil for 2-3 minutes. Loosely fill jars with hot asparagus, leaving 1-inch headspace.
4. Raw pack: Fill jars with raw asparagus, packing tightly without crushing, and leave 1-inch headspace.
5. Add 1 teaspoon of salt per quart to the jars, if desired.
6. Add boiling water, leaving 1-inch headspace.
7. Adjust lids and process according to Table 1 or Table 2.
Table 1: Recommended process time for Asparagus in a dial-gauge pressure canner.
- Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
- Process Time: 30 minutes for Pints, 40 minutes for Quarts
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 2,000 ft: 11 lb
Table 2: Recommended process time for Asparagus in a weighted-gauge pressure canner.
- Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
- Process Time: 30 minutes for Pints, 40 minutes for Quarts
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 lb; Above 1,000 ft: 15 lb
